newsSinceJun6: No material company-specific news since June 6. The dominant recent event remains the June 3 FY26 prelims (revenue +5% to GBP443.3m, adj EPS +4% to 40.3p, Q4 organic orders +14%, three acquisitions for GBP95m combined, dividend +4%, positive FY27 outlook with orders running ahead of sales), which was already incorporated into the June 8 verdicts. Today's -2.5% looks like post-results digestion/flow, not fundamental; sell-side targets (avg ~875-891p, Stifel/JPM at 1,000p) sit well above the 695p price.

sources.label: LSE RNS - discoverIE Preliminary Results (3 Jun 2026)
Proactive Investors - discoverIE starts new financial year strongly as orders surge
StockAnalysis - DSCV analyst forecast and price targets

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's move up already priced in the June 3 prelims (Q4 orders +14%, orders ahead of sales into FY27); nothing genuinely new since June 6, and today's modest -2.5% give-back with the thesis intact slightly improves entry asymmetry rather than changing the rank case.
verdicts.bull: Q4 organic orders +14% and a strong start to FY27 confirm the order-recovery inflection in an underfollowed $0.93B power/connectivity name, with consensus targets ~25-30% above the current price.
verdicts.neutral: The results are now public and partly absorbed; YTD +17.6% is moderate, leaving residual alpha but no fresh catalyst until the AGM/Q1 trading update.
verdicts.bear: Adjusted operating profit grew only 1% and margin progress is slow; if the order surge fades or the GBP95m of acquisitions disappoint, the hidden-gem rerating stalls.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q1/AGM trading update confirming orders still running ahead of sales, plus margin-target progress.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Industrial demand relapse or acquisition integration missteps undercutting the order-recovery narrative.

verdicts.rankRationale: The page's listed catalyst (order recovery) was validated by the June 3 prelims, but that evidence was already reflected in yesterday's hold; no new information since June 6 and a -2.5% drift day does not change network position.
verdicts.bull: Third consecutive quarter of organic order growth in Controls and broad demand pick-up across industrial/medical customers validate the quiet power-and-components compounding thesis.
verdicts.neutral: discoverIE remains a diversified industrial electronics compounder; edge-AI/electrification exposure is real but indirect, so it tracks industrial cycle recovery more than AI capex.
verdicts.bear: Only 1% adjusted operating profit growth and acquisition-led expansion into security dilute the AI-adjacency case; the name could stay a generic industrial recovery story.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Operating-margin target upgrade or further design-and-manufacture acquisitions in power/sensing niches.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Order momentum proving cyclical restock rather than structural electrification/edge-AI demand.

verdicts.rankRationale: The strict-screen pathway (custom sensing/control components adapted to coolant sensing and edge telemetry) is unaffected by anything since June 6; prelims confirm broad order recovery but offer no new AI-chain-specific evidence, so the speculative pathway score stands.
verdicts.bull: A healthier order book and three new acquisitions broaden the custom-electronics platform from which coolant-sensing/edge-telemetry adaptations could emerge.
verdicts.neutral: No disclosed AI-datacenter or liquid-cooling design wins; the pathway remains plausible-but-unproven, exactly as scored.
verdicts.bear: Security-market acquisitions suggest capital is being deployed away from AI-infrastructure adjacency, weakening the strict-pathway optionality.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: A disclosed design win in datacenter sensing, power conversion, or liquid-cooling instrumentation.
verdicts.primaryRisk: The AI-adjacency pathway never materializes and the name remains a pure industrial cyclical on this strict screen.
